nodes.title: Prefer high-resolution clock with fallback
nodes.why: `performance.now()` is usually better for elapsed timing, while `Date.now()` keeps compatibility.
nodes.tradeoff: This is more reliable for timing, with a bit more setup code.

nodes.title: Fail fast on invalid arguments
nodes.why: Clear early errors are easier to debug than odd behavior later in timing logic.
nodes.tradeoff: Stricter API, but fewer hidden bugs.

nodes.title: Capture start time before running work

anchor.snippet: const start = now(); const out = fn();

nodes.why: Timing should include the full work, from first execution to completion.
nodes.tradeoff: More accurate duration, especially for synchronous work.

nodes.title: Handle sync and async in one path
nodes.why: The function works the same whether `fn` returns a value or a Promise.
nodes.alternative: Provide separate APIs for sync and async tasks.
nodes.tradeoff: Single API is easier to use, with one conditional check.

nodes.title: Capture end time after completion
nodes.why: For async tasks, this ensures duration includes waiting time too.
nodes.tradeoff: Correct total duration, including async delay.

nodes.title: Clamp duration and evaluate budget
nodes.why: Clamping avoids negative values from unusual clocks, then `ok` is a simple budget comparison.
nodes.tradeoff: Slightly defensive logic, but cleaner outputs for callers.
